Historical note on title page reads: "The original version of this String Quartet by Israel's prominent woman composer... won the Béla Bartok prize in Budapest 1949. It was revised by the composer in 1971 and was awarded the Acum Prize 1971 fo the sponsoring of the publication of works. This is the first publication of this work presenting the 1971 revisions by the composer."
